questions.question: Define a rook polynomial and explain the meaning of coefficients.
keyPoints: Rook polynomial R(B,x) = Σ r_k x^k where r_k is the number of ways to place k non-attacking rooks
Coefficient of x^k represents the rook number r_k - the count of non-attacking placements for k rooks
Constant term r_0 is always 1 (one way to place zero rooks)

questions.question: Find the rook polynomial for a 2×3 board (complete board with all squares available).
keyPoints: Total squares = 2×3 = 6, so r_1 = 6
For r_2: Choose 2 rows from 2 (C(2,2)=1), choose 2 columns from 3 (C(3,2)=3), arrange in 2! ways = 1×3×2 = 6
Maximum rooks = min(2,3) = 2, so r_0 = 1
Rook polynomial = 1 + 6x + 6x²

questions.question: State and explain the product formula for rook polynomials of disjoint boards.
keyPoints: If B1 and B2 are disjoint (no shared rows or columns), then R(B1 ∪ B2, x) = R(B1, x) × R(B2, x)
This works because placements on B1 and B2 are independent
The coefficient r_k in result comes from summing products r_i from B1 and r_{k-i} from B2 for all i

questions.question: What is the maximum number of non-attacking rooks that can be placed on an m×n board? Explain.
keyPoints: Maximum = min(m, n)
Each rook occupies one row and one column
Cannot place more rooks than the smaller dimension
Proof: If m < n, can place m rooks by putting one in each row, each in different columns

questions.question: Derive the formula for r_k in a complete n×n board.
keyPoints: To place k non-attacking rooks: choose k rows from n: C(n,k) ways
Choose k columns from n: C(n,k) ways
Arrange k rooks in k×k positions: k! ways
Total: r_k = C(n,k)² × k! = n!²/(k!(n-k)!)²

questions.question: Explain the connection between rook polynomials and the Principle of Inclusion-Exclusion.
keyPoints: Rook polynomials are derived using Inclusion-Exclusion
Inclusion-Exclusion counts placements avoiding row/column conflicts
Start with all placements, subtract those with at least one conflict, add back overcounts, etc.
This gives the general formula for rook numbers

questions.question: Compute the rook polynomial for a board with 4 squares in a single row.
keyPoints: This is a 1×4 board
Can place 0 rooks: 1 way (r_0 = 1)
Can place 1 rook: 4 ways (r_1 = 4)
Cannot place 2+ rooks as they would share the same row
Rook polynomial = 1 + 4x

questions.question: Find the rook polynomial for a 3×3 complete board. Show all calculations and verify the coefficients.
keyPoints: r_0 = 1 (empty placement)
r_1 = 9 (any of 9 squares)
Rook polynomial: R_3 = 1 + 9x + 18x² + 6x³
Verify: r_2 can also be computed as number of ways to choose 2 non-attacking positions = 18

questions.question: Using the product formula, find the rook polynomial for two disjoint 2×2 boards. Verify by direct enumeration.
keyPoints: Each 2×2 board has rook polynomial: 1 + 4x + 2x²
Product: (1 + 4x + 2x²) × (1 + 4x + 2x²) = 1 + 8x + 20x² + 16x³ + 4x⁴
Verification: The combined board has 4 rows, 4 columns, 8 squares in total
Maximum rooks = min(4,4) = 4

questions.question: A board has forbidden positions in the first row and first column. Find the rook polynomial for the remaining available squares (a 2×2 board in the bottom-right corner).
keyPoints: Available board is a 2×2 board at positions (2,2), (2,3), (3,2), (3,3)
For a 2×2 complete board: r_0 = 1, r_1 = 4, r_2 = 2
Rook polynomial = 1 + 4x + 2x²
This counts placements on the available squares only
Cannot place rooks in forbidden positions (first row or column)

questions.question: Explain the deletion-contraction method for computing rook polynomials. Apply it to find R(2×2 with one corner missing).
keyPoints: Deletion-contraction: Select a square s. For deletion: compute polynomial without that square. For contraction: remove row and column containing s, compute polynomial of remaining board. Add both.
Apply to 2×2 with corner (1,1) forbidden
Deletion: R(2×2 minus corner) - compute polynomial for L-shaped board
Contraction: Remove row1 and col1, remaining is 1×1 board = 1 + x
Complete calculation gives final polynomial
